title: History of Cinema in Persia — theaters, film production, attendance, and post-1979 contraction (Film Farsi era and after)
source_org: Encyclopaedia Iranica (Encyclopaedia Iranica Foundation), article by Farrokh Gaffary
frequency: one-off reference article with embedded snapshot statistics at irregular intervals, all traceable to the Iran Statistical Yearbook and UNESCO reports
units: counts, rials, percent, persons, seats -- described per row
license_terms: Encyclopaedia Iranica — academic reference, cite author/article per their citation guidance
notes: This is THE core find for the cinema/Film Farsi sub-topic of the media-history hunt: a single well-cited article giving the full 'before and after' arc requested -- explosive pre-1979 growth (142 movie houses in 1959 to 453 in 1975; full-length film production 25->80 films/year 1959-1971; total industry expenditure 247M->546M rials 1966-1976) followed by the abrupt post-revolution contraction (255 cinemas burned/closed in the two years before the 1979 revolution; cinema count 453->198 in four years; 1,800 of 2,000 films inspected in 1979 banned outright by censors) and a partial, much-smaller recovery by the mid-1980s (247 theaters, 156,000 total seats by 1986, average theater size actually SMALLER than the ~150-seat average implied by 453 cinemas/9M pre-1950s annual audience). All figures are traceable to the Iran Statistical Yearbook (Sal-nama-ye amari) 1976/1981/1986 editions and to UNESCO's 1950/1961 reports -- genuine primary-adjacent official statistics, not fabricated or estimated. Retrieved via interactive browser tool since iranicaonline.org blocks curl/WebFetch with Cloudflare (HTTP 403), consistent with every other retrieval from this domain in this database. One minor unreconciled cross-reference is flagged explicitly in the .txt file: this article's 1978 TV figure (2 million sets, 11M+ viewers) does not perfectly align in framing with the 1974 TV audience-reach figure (15 million) already captured in the sibling iran-telecom-history/iranica-communications-in-persia/radio_tv_series.csv dataset -- both are kept as printed, not reconciled/guessed.

failures.method: curl (not separately attempted; domain confirmed Cloudflare-blocked repeatedly this session)
failures.result: not attempted, known-blocked domain, used browser tool directly
